title: compact-check negative control vs CLI telemetry: root-cause the .bee/ tree-hash mutation and fix the right side
read_first: scripts/test_compact_verbs.mjs (the [compact-check-mutates-nothing] D13 negative control — tree hash over .bee/)
skills/bee-hive/templates/bee.mjs (the direct-run timing block near the end: appends .bee/logs/timings.jsonl on EVERY invocation since wv-1)
action: ROOT-CAUSE FIRST: run the failing check in isolation and diff the fixture .bee/ tree between the two runs to name the exact mutating path(s). Expected finding: .bee/logs/timings.jsonl (wv-1 appends one line per CLI invocation — telemetry, fail-open, append-only). If confirmed: the D13 negative control's tree hash EXEMPTS .bee/logs/** (telemetry is not state; hooks.jsonl already writes there under hooks) — and to avoid the 'clearing a red by widening the threshold' trap the suite MUST gain a negative control proving detection still works: mutate a real state file (e.g. touch .bee/state.json content) between runs in a control case and assert the check goes red. If the diff names anything OTHER than logs/, that is a real compact-check state mutation — fix the mutation in the compact machinery instead and leave the suite's scope alone (then bee.mjs is untouched; drop it from --files at cap time via the recorded outcome). Twins byte-identical if bee.mjs changes. Regen: onboard --apply + impact_registry --write if registry mapping changes, release_manifest --write if any hashed file changes. Verify: test_compact_verbs green including the new negative control.

truths: root cause named from a measured tree diff, not assumption
exemption (if taken) is scoped to .bee/logs/** only and paired with a proving negative control
a genuine state mutation still turns the check red (control case green-asserts-red)
twins byte-identical if bee.mjs touched; manifest/registry regenerated as needed

verify: node scripts/test_compact_verbs.mjs && node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check && node scripts/ledger_parity.mjs --check

trace.outcome: compact-check D13 negative control fixed: measured diff showed .bee/logs/timings.jsonl (wv-1 CLI timing telemetry) plus already-exempted .bee/cache/manifest-hash.json as the only mutating paths, no real compact-check state bug. Widened HASH_EXEMPT_DIRS to cover .bee/logs/** alongside .bee/cache/**, added a proving negative control that a genuine .bee/state.json mutation still reddens the hash. bee.mjs untouched, twins stay byte-identical, no manifest/registry regen needed.
trace.verify_command: node scripts/test_compact_verbs.mjs && node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check && node scripts/ledger_parity.mjs --check
